source: ATRank- An Attention-Based User Behavior Modeling Framework for Recommendation

concepts.finding: ATRank: attention-based user behavior model for recommendation. Uses self-attention to model user behavior sequences without RNN/CNN. Positional encoding captures temporal order. Multi-head attention captures different user interests from behavior sequence. Outperforms GRU-based models by 5-8%.
concepts.implication: Self-attention over keyword sequences captures keyword intent transitions. Multi-head attention detects different intent facets (e.g., product research vs price comparison) from a keyword sequence. Attention weights explain which historical keywords influence current intent.
concepts.open_question: What sequence position encoding works best for keyword sessions? Absolute (order in session) or relative (time between keywords)?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Self-attention on keyword search sequences produces better session-level intent prediction than RNN-based sequence models (LSTM, GRU).
